name: Embalse de Mari Sánchez from Valdepeñas — circular
name_original: Embalse de Mari Sánchez desde Valdepeñas — circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Valdepeñas is characterized by expansive plains, vast vineyards, and olive groves, offering predominantly gentle terrain. The region features a network of routes across these agricultural landscapes and includes gentle hills suitable for various cycling abilities. Towards the south, the Sierra de Despeñaperros introduces more challenging, mountainous sections for experienced cyclists. The area also contains quartzite mountains and a unique volcanic outcrop, providing diverse geological features. Best touring cycling routes around Valdepeñas The most popular touring cycling route is Valdepeñas — circular route through the vineyards, an 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle ride through the region's renowned wine country. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Carlos del Valle — circular from Valdepeñas, a moderate 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route traverses plains and gentle hills, leading to cultural landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de Mari Sánchez from Valdepeñas — circular, a 19.5 miles (31.4 km) trail leading through open landscapes and past the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Valdepeñas is defined by expansive plains, vast vineyards, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through agricultural areas to more challenging routes in the southern mountains. The routes in Valdepeñas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 450 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdepeñas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Valdepeñas?
A: Valdepeñas offers a wide network of nearly 90 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various preferences, from relaxed rides through agricultural landscapes to more challenging paths in the southern mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Valdepeñas?
A: The terrain around Valdepeñas is diverse. You'll primarily find expansive plains, vast vineyards, and olive groves, offering predominantly gentle cycling. Towards the south, the Sierra de Despeñaperros introduces more challenging, mountainous sections with significant climbs for experienced cyclists. The area also features gentle hills and unique geological formations like quartzite mountains and a volcanic outcrop.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Valdepeñas?
A: The optimal seasons for touring cycling in Valdepeñas are spring (April-June) and fall (September-November). During these months, the weather is particularly pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Valdepeñas?
A: Yes, Valdepeñas offers several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. These routes typically traverse the gentle plains and agricultural areas, providing a relaxed experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058404970/embalse-de-mari-sanchez-from-valdepenas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Mari Sánchez from Valdepeñas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.5 miles (31.4 km) and leads through open landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Valdepeñas?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking more challenging terrain, routes extending towards the Sierra de Despeñaperros in the south offer significant climbs and descents. While most routes are moderate, there are 9 difficult routes available, providing a good test for advanced riders.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Valdepeñas?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Valdepeñas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056731195/valdepenas-circular-route-through-the-vineyards" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas — circular route through the vineyards&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a gentle ride through the region's wine country.

Q: What cultural or natural attractions can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can visit several cultural and natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Santísimo Cristo del Valle&lt;/a&gt; and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3024383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de San Carlos&lt;/a&gt; are notable landmarks. You can also explore the Iberian city of Cerro de Las Cabezas, the Tablas de Daimiel National Park, or the Valdepeñas Wine Museum. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3451633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Peral&lt;/a&gt; is known for its springs and accompanying trails.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that go through the famous vineyards of Valdepeñas?
A: Absolutely. Valdepeñas is renowned for its wine, and many touring cycling routes wind through the vast vineyards and olive groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056731195/valdepenas-circular-route-through-the-vineyards" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas — circular route through the vineyards&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering scenic views and opportunities to visit bodegas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Valdepeñas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Valdepeñas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the picturesque landscapes, the well-maintained paths through vineyards, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to other towns or villages from Valdepeñas?
A: Yes, some routes connect Valdepeñas to nearby towns and villages, offering a chance to explore more of the region. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057612993/san-carlos-del-valle-circular-from-valdepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Carlos del Valle — circular from Valdepeñas&lt;/a&gt; route takes you to the charming Plaza Mayor of San Carlos del Valle, known for its impressive Baroque church.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for a full day of cycling?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes that are perfect for a full day of cycling. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057718758/bolanos-de-calatrava-from-valdepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños de Calatrava from Valdepeñas&lt;/a&gt; route covers over 68 km, providing an extended touring experience through the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there routes that pass by reservoirs or other water features?
A: Yes, you can find routes that include water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058404970/embalse-de-mari-sanchez-from-valdepenas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Mari Sánchez from Valdepeñas — circular&lt;/a&gt; route leads through open landscapes and past the Mari Sánchez reservoir, offering pleasant views and a change of scenery.
tips: La Cabezuela is a recently built reservoir (80s). It is located on the Jabalón river. There are several beach areas around.

In it is the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, occupies one of the flanks of the Plaza Mayor of the town. In plan it has two Herrerian-influenced naves: the main one and San Lorenzo, which is shorter, and a Mannerist chapel on the gospel side.
In the main nave is the altarpiece, a copy of the one from 1553, destroyed in the Civil War, of which six panels have been preserved that correspond to a pre-Mannerist style prior to Correa de Vivar, which some hypotheses attribute to the Hernando Yánez School ( disciple of Raphael). At the end of this nave, the choir on a pointed arch.
In the nave of San Lorenzo, a work by Gregorio Prieto with the figure of the patron saint, the Virgin of Consolation.

It is a reconstruction of a traditional mill.
At the entrance of the town, coming from Madrid, is the Molino Gregorio Prieto, a reproduction of a traditional windmill from La Mancha. The Mill is used as a museum and houses an interesting collection of objects from the popular tradition of Valdepeñas and offers typical gastronomic products from the area.
